question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The official BLS data released today shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis, well below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution. This is definitive data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics released at 8:30 AM ET on April 10, 2026, meaning the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS March 2026 CPI data shows 0.3% increase
Release occurred today at scheduled 8:30 AM ET time
Market closes tomorrow but outcome is already determined
risks: Extremely minimal risk of data revision affecting the 0.3% vs 0.8% threshold
No meaningful uncertainty remains as official data is published
